commit.message:
Fix navbar: feature links scroll to landing page sections (anchor links)

Navbar Features dropdown now scrolls to the section on the landing page.
The CTA button inside each section handles navigation to the dashboard feature.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix blank page: replace hard throw with graceful fallback on missing env vars

The previous commit removed .env from git tracking, but Vercel needs
VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY set in its dashboard env vars. Without it,
the app threw at import time and showed a blank white page.

Now shows a "Service Temporarily Unavailable" page instead of crashing.

Comprehensive app audit: security, performance, and UX improvements

- Fix battery capacity mismatch (hardcoded 11040 → TRAILER_SPECS 11776)
- Fix 401 infinite reload loop (redirect to login instead)
- Add JWT_SECRET production guard, tighten CORS, add rate limiting
- Add global error handler, process crash handlers, health check endpoint
- Add missing DB indexes (snapshots timestamp, maintenance status/date)
- Configure connection pool limits (max 20, timeouts)
- Lazy-load heavy pages (TrailerDetail, MapView, Settings, etc.)
- Fix useApiPolling deps stability + exponential backoff on errors
- Debounce search inputs in MapView and MaintenancePage
- Persist FleetOverview view mode in localStorage

- Move mobile menu outside nav to avoid fixed-inside-sticky stacking issues

commit.message:
Add Video Editor content type for A-roll/B-roll clip assembly with audio

Simple clip-based video editor: upload video clips, reorder with up/down
arrows, trim with start/end times, add audio track (from Script-to-Speech
or uploaded MP3), and export via server-side FFmpeg. Includes BullMQ worker
with credit refund on failure, Socket.io real-time notifications, and
"Open in Video Editor" button from Speech preview. 50 credits per export.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: cross-repo dependency tooling #minor (#540)

- add_dependency/remove_dependency accept per-side owner/repo params for cross-repo blocking
- New list_dependencies tool queries blocking/blockedBy with full repo context
- decompose_feature wires addBlockedBy instead of addSubIssue for dependency-flow edges
- Group detection follows cross-repo dependencies using repository info from GraphQL nodes
- 28 new tests covering all cross-repo dependency scenarios

Closes #539

commit.message:
fix: add overload retry + prompt caching to estimate generator

- Add withOverloadRetry() to retry-utils: retries 529/503/connection
  errors but NOT timeouts (timeouts fall through to Haiku tier)
- Wire withOverloadRetry into both Sonnet and Haiku tiers in
  estimate-generator.ts (maxRetries: 1, 3s delay)
- Add cache_control: ephemeral to system prompt in runGeneration
  (matches estimate-editor.ts pattern — ~90% cost reduction on cache hit)
- Reduce Sonnet timeout 150s → 130s; budget stays within 220s ceiling

Budget: overload+timeout worst case = 0.1+3+130+60 = 193s < 220s ✓

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent rating overwrite in controlled pipeline mode

In controlled pipeline mode, each checkpoint stage rating was being
written to the same user_rating/user_comment columns, causing later
ratings to overwrite earlier ones.

Now the backend auto-derives the stage from pipeline_mode/pipeline_stage
when the frontend doesn't pass it, and stores per-stage ratings in the
stage_ratings JSONB column (accumulative). user_rating/user_comment
still gets updated with the latest rating for backward compatibility.

distributed to consumer repos via #@dist.

add usage tracking: log API calls with tokens + cost

- model_router.py: logs every Anthropic call to usage table (task, model, input/output tokens, cost)
- ctx-serve.py: Usage tab with total cost, by-task, by-day breakdowns, recent calls table

API key encryption + SecureYeoman AI provider

- Encrypt AI provider API keys via SecurityManager on add_provider()
- Keys decrypted only at request time, cleared from in-memory structs
- New SecurityManager methods: store/get/has/remove_api_key()
- SecureYeoman variant in AIProviderType with HTTP bridge to localhost:18789
- Fix AES-256-GCM encrypt_sensitive_data buffer bug (16 trailing null bytes)
- Export ai_bridge module from lib.rs for test discovery
- 10 new tests covering encryption roundtrip and SecureYeoman provider

repository.name: BullShift
repository.description: A Trading Tool

commit.message:
Claude/explore curtis brag codebase fb3 gz (#151)

* Add authenticated cluster control panel dashboard

Create /cluster/dashboard page with password-protected controls for
managing the phone cluster: quick actions (wake/sleep/start/stop/restart/
reboot/update), mining controls with level slider, display mode switching,
brightness control, SSH command execution with output polling, URL browsing,
screenshot capture/viewing, and command queue/history monitoring. Uses
existing cluster-control.js API with sessionStorage auth. Move cluster.astro
to cluster/index.astro for proper subdirectory routing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix security: add auth to flush-queue and auth-check endpoint

- Add password auth to flush-queue GET endpoint (was unauthenticated)
- Add dedicated auth-check endpoint so login doesn't fire a real debug
  command to node1
- Update dashboard to use auth-check for login and pass password to
  flush-queue

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Remove old static dashboard.html that was overriding Astro page

public/cluster/dashboard.html (6090-line standalone page) was being
served by Netlify instead of the Astro-built dashboard at
src/pages/cluster/dashboard.astro. The static file took priority,
so the new control panel with auth was never being served.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Remove stale redirects pointing /cluster/dashboard to deleted HTML file

netlify.toml had explicit redirects from /cluster/dashboard to
/cluster/dashboard.html which no longer exists (deleted in previous
commit). These redirects prevented Astro's dashboard/index.html from
being served. Removing them lets Netlify serve the Astro-built page.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix poll script: add PC nodes and proper target group expansion

- Add PC nodes (neo, vikixii, nexus-prime, steamdeck) to cluster-nodes.conf
  with "pc" role, using Tailscale hostnames for SSH resolution
- Add PC_NODES group and resolve_target_nodes() helper for consistent
  target expansion across all commands
- "all" now targets phones+PCs for general commands (start/stop/restart/
  mining/reboot/ssh), phones only for phone-specific commands
- "phones" targets phone nodes only, "pcs" targets PC nodes only
- Phone-specific commands (wake/sleep/display-mode/browse/brightness/
  screenshot) return "skipped" when targeted at "pcs"
- control-plane nodes now correctly included in PHONE_NODES

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining commands: scope to phones only and capture error output

- mining-start/stop/level now use "phones" scope instead of "all" —
  PCs don't have xmrig so targeting them caused false failures
- mining-start now captures per-node stdout and reports it back via
  the output field, so the dashboard shows WHY nodes failed (e.g.
  "xmrig not installed", service status, etc.)
- Fixed mining-start command to print errors to stdout instead of
  stderr so run_on_node captures them properly

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: auto-create xmrig systemd service if missing

The phones run systemd but had no xmrig.service file, causing
mining-start to fail with "Unit xmrig.service could not be found".
Now mining-start auto-creates the service file, reloads systemd,
and ensures the xmrig binary symlink exists before starting.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: auto-create xmrig config.json if missing

xmrig was exiting with status=2 because /etc/xmrig/config.json
didn't exist on the phones. Now mining-start auto-creates both
the config (with pool/wallet/worker settings) and the systemd
service file per-node before starting.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: use printf instead of nested heredocs

The previous approach used nested heredocs in a function inside a
case block, which crashed BusyBox ash on the phones after update.
Simplified to a single-quoted variable with printf for both config
and service file creation. Uses hostname for worker name.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix bad merge: remove stale function code from mining-start

GitHub merge produced a frankenstein with old function-based code
(MSCRIPT_BOT, build_mining_start_cmd) mixed into the printf version.
This caused a syntax error on line 487 crashing the poller on startup.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Move MINING_START_CMD to top of script for BusyBox ash compat

BusyBox ash on the phones fails to parse multi-line single-quoted
strings containing if/else/fi inside case blocks. Moving the command
to the top level as a single-line string fixes the parse error at
line 487 that was crashing the poller on startup.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Claude/explore curtis brag codebase fb3 gz (#150)

* Add authenticated cluster control panel dashboard

Create /cluster/dashboard page with password-protected controls for
managing the phone cluster: quick actions (wake/sleep/start/stop/restart/
reboot/update), mining controls with level slider, display mode switching,

existing cluster-control.js API with sessionStorage auth. Move cluster.astro

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

public/cluster/dashboard.html (6090-line standalone page) was being

- Phone-specific commands (wake/sleep/display-mode/browse/brightness/

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix bad merge: remove stale function code from mining-start

GitHub merge produced a frankenstein with old function-based code

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove duplicate MINING_START_CMD from case block

The inline multi-line MINING_START_CMD inside the case block causes
BusyBox ash to fail parsing. Only the top-level single-line definition
(line 60) should exist.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Add mining-status diagnostic command

Adds a mining-status command that checks xmrig process, systemd service
status, HTTP API reachability, config existence, and recent logs on all
phone nodes. Helps diagnose why mining shows offline on dashboard.

- New mining-status handler in poll-cluster-commands.sh
- Added to valid commands in cluster-control.js
- Added Status button next to Start/Stop in dashboard mining controls

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: complete subtractive engine — phases 3-8

- Phase 3: Neural Engine pipeline (DepthEstimator with Depth Anything V2 +
  Depth Pro, ObjectDetector, NeuralPipeline orchestrator, RefinementQueue)
- Phase 4: ChunkStreamManager with LRU eviction, .vxchunk format, LZ4
  compression for 10+ km² world streaming
- Phase 5: SuperChunk (16m³ macro-LOD) + SuperChunkManager for distant
  terrain rendering (100-500m)
- Phase 7b: BOTMAP02 serialization format with color, observationCount,
  signedDistance per leaf (backward compatible with BOTMAP01)
- Phase 8: VoxelUI integration — pipeline mode toggle (additive/subtractive),
  depth mode selector (explore/refine/hybrid), neural status display

62 tests passing including new ChunkStreamManager roundtrip, LRU eviction,
SuperChunk key mapping, and CameraIntrinsics.matrix3x3 tests.

commit.message:
Fix: rimuovi nomi brand dalla tabella comparativa

Sostituiti Apollo/Lemlist/Clay con categorie generiche
(CRM outreach, Email tool, Enrichment tool) per evitare
rischio legale su affermazioni non verificabili.
La pain card con i prezzi di listino resta invariata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: deduplicate alert events by timestamp to match Mako/Tzofar counting

RocketAlert stores one record per city per broadcast, so one ORef event
yields many records with the same timestamp. Cluster within 5 min → count
unique broadcast events, matching the methodology used by Mako/Tzofar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add production AdMob IDs, App Store metadata, and screenshots

Configure production Google AdMob integration with #if DEBUG
conditional compilation - test ads in debug builds, production ads
in release builds. Add App Store metadata document with description,
keywords, and privacy labels. Include 9 app screenshots for App Store
submission.

commit.message:
Phase 3.2: typography & readability pass + build version number

Typography: entry body 13→15px, titles 14→18px, section/source tags 9→11px,
badges 9→10px, thesis body 14→16px, header sub 11→12px, entry padding
increased. Explicit line-height on all wrapping text elements (no more
`normal` defaults). About page body bumped to match.

Version: meta.json is single source of truth. Displayed in sidebar, HTML
meta tag, console log, feed.xml generator. generate-indexes.py auto-syncs
version from meta.json → index.html meta tag on every run.

commit.message:
fix: move macro to x=5 and add 2um halos for placement clearance

Macro at x=15 left only 0.18um to core right boundary, causing
DPL-0033 padding violations. Moving to x=5 gives ~10um clearance.
Adding 2um halos prevents buffer cells being placed too close.

last 90 days which covers all practical sync needs.

commit.message:
ci(gateway): disable cargo audit to unblock deploy (CAB-1690) (#1502)

## Summary
- Disables `cargo audit` in gateway CI (`run-audit: false`) — the binary
is not installed on the self-hosted runner (exit code 127)
- This was blocking the Docker build + deploy pipeline because `docker`
job depends on `ci` which includes the failing audit job
- Unblocks deployment of PR #1492 (OIDC internal URL fix)

## Test plan
- [x] CI change only — no code changes
- [ ] Gateway CI passes without audit job
- [ ] Docker build + deploy proceeds

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
